title: Merck — Financials
html:
Module 3: Financial Architecture & Budget Intelligence

Governing Thought

Merck is a $1.8M base account targeting $2.1M in FY26 (+20% growth) — ambitious given Merck's $3B cost-cutting exercise and 6,000 job reductions. As of March 31, 2026, the account sits at $666K (32% of target) with a $671K weighted pipeline and a $766K gap to close. The financial architecture differs from J&J in a critical way: ZoomRx does not have preferred vendor pricing at Merck, meaning margins are healthier per project but competitive pricing pressure is higher. The account's revenue is concentrated around Keytruda (oncology) with growing contributions from HIV, vaccines, and transplant. Understanding Merck's franchise economics — especially the Keytruda LOE cliff (2028-2029) — is essential because Merck's own cost discipline will intensify as the cliff approaches, creating both budget pressure on existing work and urgency around new franchise investments (EyeBio, PCSK-9, IDVYNSO).

Where to Find Financial Data

System What It Holds Access Update Cadence
Kimble Revenue, project-level financials, effort tracking, invoicing Internal ZoomRx (web app) Real-time as PMs update
Cluster Management (OneDrive) Revenue/Wins/Pipeline by cluster & account; utilization OneDrive, Base 1 file Weekly refresh
SharePoint (Merck folder) Active project files, BD folder with proposals and pricing workbooks ZoomRx SharePoint Per project

Account Financial Summary

FY26 Targets & Progress

Metric Value Notes
FY25 Base $1.8M Actual revenue from prior fiscal year
FY26 Target $2.1M 20% growth — aggressive given Merck cost-cutting
FY26 YTD (3/31/26) $666K 32% of target achieved through Q1
Weighted Pipeline $671K Probability-adjusted pipeline value
Gap to Target $766K $2.1M - ($666K booked + $671K pipeline) = negative gap if pipeline converts at 100%. Realistic conversion at ~50-60% leaves a ~$300-400K shortfall
AO Dave Johnson (Principal) Account owner
AD Melissa Weinstein Day-to-day account management
Vinoth's Cluster East 7 (Chanakya, Vidhya, Madhur) Execution team

Revenue Trajectory

Period Revenue Growth Key Drivers
FY24 [Populate from Kimble] — Keytruda PET, HIV RT, Vaccines PET
FY25 $1.8M [Calculate vs FY24] Keytruda franchise, HIV expansion, Prevymis ATU
FY26 YTD (3/31) $666K — Oncology, vaccines (RSV), HIV bookings
FY26 Target $2.1M +20% vs FY25 Renewals + HIV global expansion + Gardasil PET + new wins

Revenue by Brand/TA

This is an estimated breakout based on active projects and whitespace analysis. Populate exact figures from Kimble.

Brand/TA Active Project Types Est. Annual Revenue Growth Vector
Keytruda (Oncology) NSCLC PET, HNSCC ATU, HCP-Pt Conversations, Listening Sessions, Message Testing, SubQ IO [Populate] — likely largest share Indication expansion (HNSCC, melanoma), global message testing
HIV (DOR/ISL) RT/Retainer ($125K global), SFE tracking (starting 2H26) $125K+ (growing) IDVYNSO launch SFE, additional global waves
Enflonsia (RSV) PET, Digital Tracking [Populate] — est. $80-120K Renewal + second-season tracking
Prevymis (Transplant) Kidney ATU, KT Patient Qual [Populate] — est. $60-80K Renewal + potential SOT expansion
Gardasil (Vaccines) PET (revisiting), historical VAXELIS PET [Populate] Reactivation — $80-120K if PET restarts
Lynparza (mCRPC) mCRPC PET (project #40) [Populate] Single project — limited growth
HNSCC (KN-689) HCP-Pt project (#37) [Populate] Launch tracking — potential recurring
Pan-Tumor SubQ HCP-Pt study (#39) [Populate] Cross-indication — ties to QLEX strategy

BD Pipeline Detail

Active SharePoint Projects (Last 12 Months)

# Project Name Brand/TA Status
41 Merck HIV HCP RT 2026 HIV Most recent — global RT ($125K)
40 MER_Lynparza_mCRPC_PET_2026 Oncology Active
39 Merck Pan-Tumor SubQ HCP-Pt Oncology (cross) Active
38 Merck Enflonsia Digital Tracking RSV/Vaccines Active
37 Merck HNSCC HCP-Pt Project Oncology Active
36 MER_clesrovimab PET_2025_2026 RSV/Vaccines Active
35 MER HIV DOR+ISL RT Sampling 2025 HIV Active
29 Merck Keytruda NSCLC PET Oncology Active
26 MER Prevymis Kidney ATU Transplant Active

BD Folder Contents (22 Items — Recent Proposals)

Proposal Brand Est. Value Status
ZoomRx Proposal - Global HIV DOR+ISL HCP Agile Research (Mar 2026) HIV $125K WON — first global win at Merck
ZoomRx_Keytruda Message Testing and Optimization V2 (Apr 2026) Keytruda [Populate] In pipeline
MER_HIV_islatravir_Global_RT_2026 Intake HIV [Populate] In pipeline
ZoomRx Solid Tumor SubQ IO HCP-Patient Conversations Proposal (Aug 2025) Keytruda (cross) [Populate] Active project
ZoomRx Agile Research - Merck Zerbaxa (Aug 2025) Infectious Disease [Populate] [Check status]
ZoomRx Proposal - Cardio SFE (Jan 2025) Cardiology [Populate] [Check status — may have died]

Pricing workbooks on file for: Keytruda, HIV, Prevymis, Lenvima, Enflonsia, Winrevair

Gap Analysis: Path to $2.1M

The Math

FY26 Target:                    $2,100K
FY26 YTD Booked (3/31/26):    -$  666K
                               --------
Remaining to close:             $1,434K

Weighted pipeline:              $  671K
Realistic conversion (60%):     $  403K
                               --------
True gap (after conversion):    $1,031K

Renewals floor (est.):         -$  415K  (Keytruda PET + HIV retainer + Enflonsia + Prevymis)
                               --------
Net new wins needed:            $  616K

Revenue Scenarios

Scenario Description FY26 Total Gap
Bear case Renewals only, no new wins ~$1.5M -$600K (miss target by 29%)
Base case Renewals + Gardasil PET + partial pipeline conversion ~$1.8-1.9M -$200-300K (miss by 10-14%)
Bull case Renewals + Gardasil PET + Keytruda expansion + EyeBio entry ~$2.0-2.2M At or near target

Realistic assessment: The 20% growth target is ambitious given Merck's cost-cutting environment. Base case is likely — expect stabilization at $1.8-1.9M with growth potential deferred to 2H26 and FY27.

Pricing Intelligence

Pricing Benchmarks (from BD Folder)

Study Type Brand Approx. Price Point Notes
Global HIV RT HIV/DOR+ISL $125K First global win — potential template for future global work
PET (single brand, US) Various $80-150K Depends on sample size and wave count
Digital Tracking Enflonsia [Populate from pricing workbook] Passive tracking — lower cost, recurring
ATU (single wave) Prevymis [Populate] One-off quant surveys
HCP-Pt Conversations Keytruda [Populate from SubQ IO proposal] Higher cost — qual + quant hybrid
Message Testing Keytruda [Populate from V2 proposal] Global + US variants

Pricing Dynamics at Merck

  • No preferred vendor discount (unlike J&J) — ZoomRx prices at standard rates, which means healthier margins per project but more price competition from firms like Spherix, InCrowd, and DRG
  • Merck procurement is active — cost-cutting mandate means every MR purchase is scrutinized. Lead proposals with ROI justification.
  • AI addendum blocks AI-enabled pricing — cannot charge premium for AI capabilities until MSA addendum is in place
  • BrandImpact historically competitive at Merck — they have an installed base. ZoomRx wins on Keytruda depth and tracking methodology, not on price.

Merck's Franchise Economics (Context for Account Planning)

Understanding Merck's corporate financial dynamics is critical for anticipating MR budget behavior.

Keytruda — The $31.7B Franchise Under Siege

Metric Value Implication for MR Budget
FY2025 Revenue ~$31.7B >50% of Merck's total pharma revenue
Growth Rate +7% YoY (decelerating from +18% in FY24) Slower growth = more budget scrutiny on marketing
LOE Timeline Composition-of-matter patent expires 2028 Biosimilar entry 2028-2029 — Merck is in defensive mode
QLEX Strategy 30-40% patient transition target by 2028 New patent estate — Merck investing heavily in SubQ switch
Perioperative Expansion KN-671 (NSCLC), KN-689 (HNSCC), EV-302 (bladder) New patient pools = new research needs = ZoomRx opportunity
IRA Price Negotiation Keytruda selected Price cuts from CMS add urgency to biosimilar defense

Implication: Keytruda generates the most research demand at Merck — but the LOE cliff means Merck will scrutinize every Keytruda MR dollar more aggressively. Position ZoomRx work as essential to the LOE defense strategy (QLEX adoption tracking, perioperative launch monitoring, competitive defense).

Non-Keytruda Franchises

Franchise Revenue Growth Outlook MR Budget Signal
HIV (IDVYNSO) ~$1.8B projected by 2033 High growth — launch May 2026 Fresh budget allocation for launch tracking. ZoomRx already embedded ($125K global RT).
Gardasil $5.2B (FY25, -39%) Declining (China collapse) Budget contracting. Gardasil PET revisit may be defensive (maintain franchise visibility during downturn).
Prevymis $978M (FY25, +25%) Strong growth — KT expansion Growing budget. Approaching $1B — Merck will invest in market monitoring.
Enflonsia/RSV Launch year (FY25) Growing — competitive with Beyfortus Active investment in PET + digital. Budget should sustain.
EyeBio Pipeline Future New budget when launch approaches — currently speculative.
PCSK-9 Pipeline Future Same as EyeBio — park unless signal emerges.
Winrevair (PAH) Launch year Growing Pricing workbook exists — ZoomRx has foot in door.

Watch Items

Merck Cost-Cutting Impact on MR Budget (HIGH)

The $3B cost-cutting exercise with 6,000 job reductions is the single most important financial context for the account. MR budgets are being scrutinized across all franchises. This is not hypothetical — it has already depressed 1H26 bookings.

Mitigation: Lead every proposal with cost-efficiency framing. Highlight ZoomRx's existing institutional knowledge (reduces onboarding cost), recurring study efficiencies (wave-over-wave cost compression), and multi-brand synergies (cross-franchise insights from single vendor).

AI Addendum Blocker (HIGH)

The Merck MSA requires an AI addendum before any AI-enabled offerings can be sold. This blocks Dynamic Dialogues, Sagan follow-up quals, AI-powered moderation, and any AI-differentiated pricing. Until resolved, ZoomRx cannot differentiate on the one capability that justifies premium pricing in a cost-cutting environment.

Action: Dave/Melissa must prioritize this with Merck procurement. This is the single highest-leverage BD action on the account.

Keytruda LOE Revenue Cliff (MEDIUM-HIGH)

Keytruda's composition-of-matter patent expires 2028. If Merck's Keytruda revenue declines sharply (70-80% biosimilar erosion pattern), the MR budget for Keytruda-related work could contract dramatically by FY29-30. ZoomRx needs to diversify its Merck revenue base toward non-Keytruda franchises (HIV, vaccines, transplant, pipeline) before the cliff arrives.

Action: Track non-Keytruda revenue as a % of total Merck revenue quarterly. Target >40% by FY28.

Two-BU Budget Fragmentation (MEDIUM)

The restructuring into Merck Oncology and Merck Specialty creates two separate buying centers with distinct budgets. This is both risk (a project that crosses BUs requires two sign-offs) and opportunity (two budgets to sell into independently).

Action: Ensure proposals are BU-specific. Cross-BU work (e.g., SubQ IO spanning Keytruda and Winrevair) needs explicit budget ownership clarification.

Financial Questions to Answer

Revenue & Pipeline (Pull from Kimble)

  • What is Merck's trailing-12-month revenue to ZoomRx? Breakout by brand.
  • What are the top 5 Merck projects by revenue? Which brands do they belong to?
  • What is the current pipeline (proposals submitted but not yet won)? Decision timelines?
  • What Merck SOWs expire in the next 90 days and need renewal?
  • What is the win rate on Merck proposals over the last 2 years?

Profitability

  • What is the blended margin on Merck work? (Should be higher than J&J given no preferred vendor discount.)
  • Which Merck projects are running over-effort?
  • Is scope creep happening on any active project?

Cluster Context

  • What share of East 7's total revenue comes from Merck?
  • Are Chanakya, Vidhya, and Madhur at >85% utilization on Merck + other work?
  • If a new Merck win comes in (e.g., Gardasil PET), does East 7 have capacity or does Vinoth need to staff up?

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
